extractions.problem: Precise reagent dispensing and cell culture on digital microfluidic devices is limited by active electrostatic methods.
extractions.method: Teflon-AF liftoff protocol for patterning hydrophilic sites on two-plate DMF devices to create virtual microwells for passive dispensing.
extractions.finding: Reproducible dispensing of 80–800 nL volumes with 0.7–13.8% CV; passive dispensing outperforms active electrostatic dispensing and is compatible with adherent cell culture.
extractions.metric: CV (coefficient of variation), dispensed volume range
extractions.result: CV 0.7%–13.8% for 80–800 nL volumes

extractions.problem: Programming and safe monitoring of industrial robots requires dynamic updates to a virtual cell model.
Frequent handovers and heavy signaling overhead degrade mobility management in ultra-dense networks, especially for fast-moving vehicles.
extractions.method: Proactive mobility management using virtual cell technique with LSTM-based trajectory prediction in an SDN controller.
extractions.finding: The proactive solution eliminates handover delay and reduces handover signaling cost by 35% compared to the reactive approach.
extractions.metric: Handover signaling cost, handover delay, prediction accuracy
extractions.result: 35% signaling cost reduction

extractions.problem: Cell formation and production planning in dynamic environments where product mix and demand change over time, requiring reconfiguration.
extractions.method: Fuzzy goal programming for multi-objective optimization of virtual cellular manufacturing with worker flexibility over multi-period planning horizons.
extractions.finding: The model minimizes holding cost, backorder cost, and exceptional elements in a machine–part–worker incidence matrix under dynamic demand.
extractions.metric: Holding cost, backorder cost, exceptional elements

extractions.problem: Load imbalance among cells in LTE-A systems degrades system performance.
extractions.method: Load Prediction based Virtual Cell Breathing (LPVCB) scheme that uses load prediction and cell individual offset (CIO) control for load balancing.
extractions.finding: The proposed scheme improves system performance through more accurate load balancing using predictive virtual cell breathing.

extractions.problem: Directional channel modeling in microcells requires both deterministic and stochastic propagation simulation.
extractions.method: Virtual Cell Deployment Areas (VCDA) with cluster tracing: geometrical layouts define typical deployment areas, then multipath clusters are traced geometrically with stochastic small-scale fading.
extractions.finding: The approach was adopted by the COST259 DCM standard model for directional wireless channels.
extractions.result: Adopted as COST259 DCM standard

extractions.problem: Determining the size of the active site in proteases, specifically papain.
Analyzing the mechanical behavior of woven fibrous materials at the unit cell level.
extractions.method: Virtual tests at the unit cell level for mechanical behavior analysis.

extractions.problem: Evaluating different lot sizing splitting strategies for virtual cells in distributed manufacturing layouts.
extractions.method: Computational simulation comparing three lot splitting strategies: no splitting, equally balanced splitting to K virtual cells, and unbalanced splitting using a makespan-based procedure.
extractions.finding: Separating parts to virtual cells significantly reduces makespan; the proposed unbalanced method performs better when total machines and part input orderings are considered.
extractions.result: Significant makespan reduction

extractions.problem: Existing cell formation approaches neglect layout and handling strategies, failing to relate layout decisions to machine grouping and sharing.
extractions.method: A new cell formation approach integrating machine grouping and hybrid cellular layout design using overlapping GT cells, functional layout, and virtual manufacturing cells.
extractions.finding: Combining overlapping GT cells with functional layout reduces the need for machine duplication among cells, challenging traditional emphasis on independent cells.
extractions.dataset: Two illustrative manufacturing examples from literature

extractions.problem: Wi-Fi Direct is not effective for spreading data over wide areas due to random partner selection and access control wait times.
extractions.method: Novel information diffusion method combining Wi-Fi Direct with DTN, using virtual cells based on device locations and a reaction–diffusion mathematical model for performance evaluation.
extractions.finding: The proposed virtual cell-based method enables efficient interference-free information diffusion; the reaction–diffusion model provides quantitative performance evaluation.

extractions.problem: 5G vehicular networks suffer from frequent handovers and connection drops due to ultra-dense small cells and high mobility.
extractions.method: Connectivity-oriented SARSA Reinforcement Learning (CO-SRL) algorithm for user-centric virtual cell management and handover reduction.
extractions.finding: CO-SRL reduced the number of handovers and cumulative handover time in realistic vehicular simulations.
extractions.metric: Number of handovers, cumulative handover time
extractions.result: Reduced handovers and cumulative handover time

extractions.problem: Co-channel interference among sectors in relay enhanced cellular systems degrades performance.
extractions.method: Joint adaptive soft frequency reuse and virtual cell power control (JAP) algorithm for LTE-A relay enhanced cellular systems, using SIR-balance principle.
extractions.finding: The algorithm achieves frequency reuse factor of one among sectors and obtains both high average spectral efficiency and edge user throughput.
extractions.dataset: LTE-A relay enhanced cellular system simulation
extractions.metric: Average spectral efficiency, edge user throughput
extractions.result: High spectral efficiency and edge user throughput

extractions.problem: Optimizing spectral efficiency and energy efficiency in bidirectional distributed antenna systems with interference.
extractions.method: CCCP algorithm for non-convex power allocation optimization in bidirectional DAS with and without user-centric virtual cells, using D.C. programming and fractional programming.
extractions.finding: Bidirectional DAS with user-centric virtual cells achieves much better SE and EE than without virtual cells.
extractions.baseline: Bidirectional DAS without user-centric virtual cells
extractions.result: SE and EE much better with virtual cells

extractions.problem: GAF algorithm's square grid-based equivalence does not match true radio diffusion, limiting energy conservation efficiency in wireless sensor networks.
extractions.method: Hexagonal virtual cell structure for GAF algorithm grouping, exploiting hexagonal geometry closer to radio diffusion patterns.
extractions.finding: The proposed algorithm wakes up only ~52% of nodes compared to GAF while maintaining network connectivity, with only 1.16% additional packet delay.
extractions.metric: Active node ratio, packet delay, network connectivity
extractions.result: 50.69% nodes awake vs GAF, +1.16% packet delay

extractions.problem: Spectral efficiency and energy efficiency optimization in distributed antenna systems with virtual cells.
Creating and managing user-centric virtual cells in ultra-dense 5G networks for high-mobility vehicle-to-network communications.
extractions.method: Vehicle-centric probabilistic approach using speed criteria and complex network metrics for virtual cell selection, processed in a centralized controller, simulated in ns3.
extractions.finding: The proposed approach enables more assertive virtual cell selection, improving IoV services through 5G networks.
extractions.dataset: ns3 simulation of 5G ultra-dense vehicular network
extractions.result: More assertive virtual cell selection

extractions.problem: Understanding the dynamic behavior of cells requires computational whole-cell models, which are becoming feasible with accumulating -omics data.
extractions.method: The E-CELL project constructs whole cell simulation models integrating genome, proteome, transcriptome, and metabolome data.
extractions.finding: Computer simulations of virtual cell models have revealed biological surprises; whole-cell simulation is identified as a grand challenge of the 21st century.

extractions.problem: Naive T cell responses erode with aging, but the mechanisms involving true naive vs virtual memory T cell precursors are not fully understood.
extractions.method: Analysis of TCR transgenic and wild-type old mice examining virtual memory (VM) CD8 T cell conversion, proliferation, and apoptosis in response to TCR and cytokine stimulation.
extractions.finding: Two separate age-related defects—reduction in true naive T cell precursors and impaired proliferative capacity of VM cells—combine to reduce naive T cell responses with aging; VM conversion depends on endogenous TCRα rearrangements.
extractions.dataset: TCR transgenic and wild-type old/young mice
extractions.metric: Proliferation rate, apoptosis rate, Ag-specific precursor frequency
extractions.result: ≥70% loss of Ag-specific CD8 T cell precursors in old mice

extractions.problem: Cell-free massive MIMO requires excessive backhaul overhead as all access points serve all users.
extractions.method: User-centric (UC) virtual cell approach where each user is served by a limited number of nearby access points in a cell-free massive MIMO system.
extractions.finding: The UC approach requires less backhaul overhead and outperforms the cell-free approach in achievable rate-per-user for the vast majority of users.
extractions.result: Outperforms CF in rate-per-user for majority of users

extractions.problem: User mobility causes frequent handovers and data rate degradation in 5G networks; rapid decisions are needed for virtual cell enable/disable.
extractions.method: RNN with Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) for predicting RSS values and triggering conditions for enabling/disabling CoMP-based virtual cell mode.
extractions.finding: The GRU-RNN model achieves 92% accuracy in predicting VC mode triggering conditions, enabling proactive virtual cell management.
extractions.dataset: Simulated 5G CoMP network with user mobility
extractions.metric: Prediction accuracy, data rate, handover count
extractions.result: 92% prediction accuracy

extractions.problem: Understanding the frequency behavior and mode competition in virtual-cathode oscillators.
extractions.method: Time-frequency analysis of microwave radiation from a virtual-cathode oscillator, with particle-in-cell simulations using MAGIC code.
extractions.finding: Clear evidence of mode hopping and frequency shifting was observed; dependence of mode competition on diode operation parameters was identified; simulations agreed with experiments.
extractions.result: Simulation results in general agreement with experiments

extractions.problem: Reducing energy consumption in overlapping coverage areas of cellular networks.
extractions.method: Virtual cell clustering (VCC) technique that aggregates spare bandwidth from adjacent cells with a service partition optimization procedure.
extractions.finding: The proposed cooperative VCC scheme outperforms existing single-cell serving mode with significant energy saving.
extractions.dataset: Simulated cellular network with overlapping coverage
extractions.result: Significant energy saving over existing mode

extractions.problem: Modeling feedbacks and oscillations in virtual cell systems.
Optimizing power allocation and user association for highly mobile vehicular edge networks in highway scenarios.
extractions.method: Joint user association and power allocation using machine learning; a distributed SARL-MARL reinforcement learning algorithm for virtual cell formation.
extractions.finding: SARL-MARL obtains nearly genie-aided optimal solutions and outperforms existing schemes for vehicular edge networks.
extractions.result: Nearly identical to genie-aided optimal

extractions.problem: Location privacy threats in ultra-dense 5G networks due to tracking attacks.
extractions.method: Analysis of the effect of mobile/access node densification and virtual cell aggregation on location privacy through simulation.
extractions.finding: Virtual cells can statistically reduce tracked mobile nodes by up to 10%; tracking attack success inversely relates to the number of moving nodes.
extractions.result: Up to 10% reduction in tracked nodes

extractions.problem: Ultra-dense 5G networks face complex interference and high handover frequency; virtual cell design must balance data rates and resource overhead.
extractions.method: Fuzzy logic-based virtual cell design with stochastic geometry modeling and a fuzzy logic-based activation strategy selection (FASS) algorithm for threshold and resource allocation.
extractions.finding: The proposed solution effectively eliminates interference, improves system spectral efficiency, and dynamically adapts to different traffic loads and QoS requirements.
extractions.dataset: Simulated ultra-dense 5G network with imperfect CSI
extractions.result: Improved spectral efficiency with dynamic adaptation

extractions.problem: Existing Wi-Fi Direct methods randomly select communication partners, limiting information diffusion in disaster areas.
extractions.method: Novel information diffusion method using Wi-Fi Direct with virtual cells based on device location information, enabling interference-free scheduled communication.
extractions.finding: The scheduling control of the proposed method outperforms conventional methods in information diffusion effectiveness.
extractions.result: Outperforms conventional methods

extractions.problem: Real-time control of dynamic clamp experiments requires fast, flexible simulation of nonlinear membrane conductances in living cells.
extractions.method: Real-Time Linux Dynamic Clamp system that simulates dozens of nonlinear conductances (deterministic and stochastic ion channels) at clock rates above 10 kHz.
extractions.finding: The system handles complex models including spatially extended excitable structures and multiple interacting cells in hard real-time, with freely available software.
extractions.result: Clock rates well above 10 kHz

extractions.problem: Understanding virtual memory CD8 T cells as a memory-like but antigen-naïve population and their role in immune heterogeneity.
extractions.method: Review and synthesis of literature on TVM cell generation, maintenance, memory-like hallmarks, and signaling pathways during steady state, infection, and aging.
extractions.finding: TVM cells are semi-differentiated antigen-naïve CD8 T cells with memory-like hallmarks; a model integrating key signaling pathways during their generation is proposed.
extractions.dataset: Published studies on CD8 TVM cells

extractions.problem: Distributed radio resource management in infrastructure-less MANETs requires efficient channel allocation without centralized control.
extractions.method: Shared grid of virtual cells with pre-assigned communication channels using orthogonal frequency hopping patterns for clusters of 3 and 7 virtual cells.
extractions.finding: Average SINR is compared for random waypoint mobility model with two virtual cell designs; virtual cells enable legacy cellular reuse schemes in MANETs.
extractions.dataset: Simulated MANET with random waypoint mobility

extractions.problem: Need for a general framework for simulating and visualizing cellular physiology.
Dynamic formation of virtual manufacturing cells requires accurate matching of component processing requirements to available machining capabilities.
extractions.method: Concurrent process and facility prototyping using generic 'resource elements' in a four-step methodology: requirement analysis, capability boundary definition, machine tool selection, and system evaluation.
extractions.finding: The methodology facilitates dynamic formation of virtual manufacturing structures by accurately assessing and matching processing requirements to existing facilities.

extractions.problem: Screening and designing cell-penetrating peptides (CPPs) for drug delivery is time-consuming experimentally.
extractions.method: Computer-aided virtual screening using CellPPD, an in silico tool for prediction, screening, and designing of CPPs.
extractions.finding: In silico screening followed by experimental validation is a reliable, less time-consuming, and cost-effective approach for CPP development.

extractions.problem: The original 2D virtual cell embedding (VCE) cartesian gridding method is inadequate for axisymmetric geometries.
extractions.method: Simple extension of VCE to handle axisymmetric cases by computing radial coordinates of cells and interfaces, tested on conical flow simulations.
extractions.finding: The extension achieves accuracy consistent with basic VCE surface representation in planar geometries and is useful for practical engineering design.
extractions.result: Accuracy consistent with planar VCE

extractions.problem: Taxonomy and review of past research on virtual manufacturing cells to identify future research issues.
Virtual cell concept applied to mobile computer communications.
Simulation and experiment paradigm for virtual cell modeling in cell biology.
Cell biology modeling requires physically consistent, mathematically rigorous spatial simulation frameworks.
extractions.method: Virtual Cell framework with separated layers for biological models, physical mechanisms, geometry, mathematical models, and numerical methods, with remote database and cluster computation.
extractions.finding: The separation of layers reduces software complexity and clarifies modeling decisions; the framework provides scalable spatial modeling and simulation for cell biology.

extractions.problem: Biology education needs immersive virtual environments for teaching cellular biology and scientific reasoning.
extractions.method: JavaMOO-based Virtual Cell desktop immersive virtual environment with deployable server architectures, robust simulations, and high-quality animations.
extractions.finding: The Virtual Cell platform addresses technical challenges in biology education including server architecture, simulation robustness, and interactive animations.

extractions.problem: Scheduling of virtual manufacturing cells (VMCs) is a complex optimization problem.
extractions.method: Genetic algorithm based heuristic for VMC scheduling.

extractions.problem: Analysis of single molecule imaging experiments is complicated by stochastic events, instrument noise, and limited per-molecule information; cross-checking with simulation is needed.
extractions.method: Object-oriented 3D virtual cell model simulating single molecule dynamics (movements, binding events) with realistic fluorescence imaging output including noise and emission fluctuations.
extractions.finding: The model accurately reproduces reported single molecule experiments and can predict outcomes of future experiments with realistic simulated fluorescence video data.
extractions.result: Accurately reproduces reported experiments

extractions.problem: Analyzing downlink ergodic rate in virtual cell-based Cloud Radio Access Networks (CRAN) with cooperative RRH transmission.
extractions.method: Stochastic geometry model with Poisson point processes for RRH and MS locations; derived tractable integral expression for downlink ergodic rate of (N,K) virtual cell-based CRAN.
extractions.finding: In interference-limited regime, ergodic rate depends only on MS-to-RRH intensity ratio; cooperation among multiple RRHs significantly improves downlink ergodic rate.
extractions.dataset: Simulated CRAN with Poisson-distributed RRHs and MSs
extractions.baseline: Conventional cellular networks (single RRH per cell)
extractions.result: Significant ergodic rate improvement with RRH cooperation

extractions.problem: Determining under what conditions virtual cell manufacturing layouts are advantageous compared to job shop and traditional cellular layouts.
extractions.method: Simulation and design of experiments comparing virtual cell, job shop, and cellular layouts with identical capacities under the same demand, using priority-based production sequencing.
extractions.finding: Virtual cell layout performance depends on specific conditions; a significant number of factors and levels were tested to delimit the scope where virtual cells lead to competitive advantages.
extractions.dataset: Simulated manufacturing systems with identical capacities

extractions.problem: Resource allocation in cellular networks needs an approach between fully centralized and fully decentralized optimization.
extractions.method: Fog/neighborhood-based optimization using virtual cells of multiple base stations, with hierarchical clustering for cell formation and several methods for NP-hard joint channel access and power allocation.
extractions.finding: Proper design of fog optimization results in little degradation relative to centralized optimization even with many virtual cells; improper design leads to significant sum rate decrease.
extractions.result: Little degradation vs centralized with proper design
